headline.resting: Lean back and rest a moment
headline.bad: Take a moment — straighten your shoulders

subline.paused: Resume to start checking your posture again
subline.away: We'll resume automatically when you return
subline.resting: We'll resume automatically when you sit up straight
subline.standing: Standing straightens your spine. Try raising both arms for a light stretch.

aiGuide.none: No posture issues detected today yet! You're maintaining excellent posture. Periodic light stretching is all you need.
aiGuide.forwardHead: Forward head and sitting too close to the screen are your main issues. Raise your monitor 5–10 cm so your eye line meets the top third of the screen. This lowers tension in the splenius muscles and helps prevent cervical arthritis.
aiGuide.slouching: Slouching was detected often. Press your pelvis fully against the chair back and keep your knees at 90°. Maintaining lumbar lordosis can cut disc pressure by more than half.
aiGuide.chinResting: Chin resting was observed somewhat. It's a habit that promotes carpal tunnel syndrome and shoulder imbalance. Rest both elbows lightly on the desk to distribute the load ergonomically.
aiGuide.generic: {{label}} was detected most often. After 50 minutes of focus, always loosen your shoulders for 1 minute and add light full-body stretching to relieve joint pressure.

health.label: Posture health score
health.tooltip: Today's posture health score and grade, calculated from real-time detection data. (S/A/B/C/D grading)
health.ground: Linked to the Nachemson spinal mechanics model and the American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons (AAOS) alignment criteria.

violations.label: Posture violations
violations.tooltip: How often posture violations such as forward head, slouching, and chin resting were detected during today's real-time monitoring.
violations.ground: Harvard Medical School guideline: cumulative posture-violation exposure causes static muscle fatigue, raising pain-syndrome risk by 3.1×.

stretch.label: Stretch score
stretch.tooltip: Today's score for resetting static spinal load with chest openers, shoulder stretches, and more.
stretch.ground: After 50 minutes of sitting, 1 minute of stretching restores local oxygen supply by 25% and fully prevents lactic-acid buildup — a skeletal-muscle reset therapy.

usage.label: Total time today
usage.tooltip: Total active work time during which the real-time posture analysis engine ran to precisely protect your spinal health.
usage.ground: Feedback behavioral-intervention research: workers given alerts and visual feedback recover good posture 4.2× more proactively than those without.

D.label: At risk
D.desc: Risk of chronic pain and neck stiffness

camera.helpBody1: When your camera angle changes (laptop/monitor, etc.), body recognition can distort geometrically. We recommend setting a new baseline to prevent misdetection.
camera.helpBody2: 💡 Register each angle (front/left/right) just once, and afterward it auto-recognizes in real time even if the camera moves — no extra setup needed!

report.basisTitle: Scientific spinal-mechanics basis for the posture health grade
report.basisBody: The grade classification (S/A/B/C/D) in this report is deeply based on Nachemson's spinal biomechanics research on intradiscal pressure and the NASA Neutral Body Posture guideline, designed with real-time posture-sensing data. With slumped alignment (grades C·D), the vertical disc load rises to 1.5×–2.2× (150%–220%) versus standing, and forward head applies up to 27 kg of moment shear load on the cervical spine. The index improvement and faster-reaction metrics in this report fully demonstrate how well harmful static load on the spine is being relieved.
report.trendTitle: Posture health score trend by hour (today)
report.legendTitle: Grade legend (linked to Nachemson spinal-mechanics weighting):
report.proGift1: • Personalized clinical advice based on real-time detection data
report.proGift2: • Daily/weekly/monthly trend reports of neck angle and shoulder balance
report.proGift4: • Desktop app integration (native background + widget included)

clinicalWarn.forward_head: Loss of cervical lordosis adds up to 27 kg of load on the neck bones and causes forward-head (tech neck) syndrome.
clinicalWarn.chin_resting: Causes facial asymmetry and uneven pressure on the TMJ disc, which can progress to joint disorders.
clinicalWarn.shoulder_tilt: Applies biased load on the shoulder axis, causing pelvic displacement and scoliosis.
clinicalWarn.slouching: Collapses lumbar lordosis, increasing the risk of a herniated disc by more than 3×.
clinicalWarn.monitor_too_close: Causes severe fatigue of the eye-focusing muscles and unconscious over-tension of the neck muscles.
clinicalWarn.shoulder_asymmetry: Promotes asymmetric trapezius tension, causing chronic shoulder knots and damage to neck muscle mechanics.
clinicalWarn.head_roll: A direct trigger for biased wear of the cervical facet joints and shoulder myofascial pain syndrome.

biomechDetail.forward_head: When the cervical spine shifts forward, about 4.5 kg of extra load occurs on the neck joints, causing chronic forward-head syndrome.
biomechDetail.slouching: Slumped spinal alignment surges lumbar vertical disc pressure to over 150% of normal, inducing back pain and disc damage.
biomechDetail.shoulder_tilt: One-sided shoulder drop causes clavicular asymmetry and severe imbalanced tension in both trapezius muscles (myofascial pain).
biomechDetail.chin_resting: Applies uneven shear force to the TMJ, which can cause disc displacement and asymmetric facial shift.
biomechDetail.monitor_too_close: Sitting too close to the monitor causes sustained tension of the eye-focusing muscles, leading to dry eye and accommodative myopia.
biomechDetail.shoulder_asymmetry: A representative skeletal-asymmetry warning that causes one-sided compressive stress on the lumbosacral joint, linked to pelvic misalignment.
biomechDetail.head_roll: Causes imbalance of the splenius and small muscles around the cervical spine, raising the risk of cervical arthritis.
biomechDetail.default: We recommend maintaining neutral spinal alignment by adjusting your chair and monitor heights ergonomically.

report.noViolationOnDate: 👏 No {{label}} violations were recorded on the selected date!
report.proOnlyTitle: The precision analysis report is a PRO feature
report.proOnlyDesc: Based on today's accumulated data, **clinical physical-therapy and ergonomic AI advice feedback** and **second-level precision timestamp logs** are advanced features available only to PRO members.
